answers.answer_text: The vinegar adds elasticity to the egg's membrane

answers.answer_text: The egg undergoes a phase change to become solid rubber

answers.answer_text: The calcium carbonate shell dissolves, leaving only the flexible inner membrane

answers.text: Calcium acetate (Ca(CH₃COO)₂)
Calcium chloride (CaCl₂)
Calcium hydroxide (Ca(OH) ₂)
Calcium sulfate (CaSO₄)

question text: Which of the following is NOT a product of the reaction between calcium carbonate and acetic acid?

answers.text: Carbon dioxide
Sodium chloride
Water
Calcium acetate

question text: Is the reaction between the eggshell (calcium carbonate) and vinegar (acetic acid) fast or slow?

answers.answer_text: Fast, because carbon dioxide bubbles form rapidly at the start
answers.text: Fast, because carbon dioxide bubbles form rapidly at the start

answers.answer_text: Fast, because the reaction produces immediate results

answers.answer_text: Slow, because vinegar is a weak acid and does not react with calcium carbonate

answers.answer_text: Slow, because it takes several days for the shell to fully dissolve
